entries.definition:
كتاب الرّاء

ركب

(ركد) الراء والكاف والدال أصلٌ يدلُّ على سُكون. يقال ركَدَ الماءُ: سكَنَ. وركَدتِ الرِّيحُ. وركَد الميزان: استَوَى. وركد القومُ رُكوداً: سكنُوا وهدَؤوا. وجَفنَةٌ رَكود: مملوءة. فأمّا قولُهم تراكَدَ الجوارِي، إذا قعدَتْ إحداهُنّ على قدميها ثم نَزَتْ قاعدةً إِلى صاحبتها، فهذا إن صحَّ فهو شاذٌّ عن الأصل.

رَكَّبَ rakkaba [v. II, trans.] to compose, to put together (82:8) فِىٓ أَىِّ صُورَةٍ مَّا شَآءَ رَكَّبَكَ in whatever form He chooses, He composes you.
مُتَرَاكِب mutarākib [act. part. of v. VI تَرَاكَبَ tarākaba] to stack up on top of one another, to cluster, to be in rows (6:99) فَأَخْرَجْنَا بِهِۦ نَبَاتَ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ فَأَخْرَجْنَا مِنْهُ خَضِرًا نُّخْرِجُ مِنْهُ حَبًّا مُّتَرَاكِبًا then with it We produce the shoots of everything [every plant], then from it We bring forth vegetation, from which We produce grains in close-stacked rows.

رَكْب rakb [coll. n.] travelling party, group of travelling camel-riders, caravan (8:42) إِذْ أَنتُم بِٱلْعُدْوَةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَهُم بِٱلْعُدْوَةِ ٱلْقُصْوَىٰ وَٱلرَّكْبُ أَسْفَلَ مِنكُمْ [remember] when you were on the near side [of the valley], and they were on the far side, and the caravan was below you.

رُكْبَان rukbān [pl. of coll. n. ركب rakb/pl. of quasi-act. part. راكب rākib] riders, riding, being on a camel or horseback (2:239) فَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ فَرِجَالًا أَوْ رُكْبَانًا if you are in a state of fear [from an enemy], then [pray] on foot or while riding.

رِكَاب rikāb [coll. n.] mounts, particularly camels (59:6) وَمَآ أَفَآءَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ رَسُولِهِۦ مِنْهُمْ فَمَآ أَوْجَفْتُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ خَيْلٍ وَلَا رِكَابٍ whatever God gave as spoils to His Messenger from them [his opponents], [are those for which] you did not have [in the course of capturing them] to spur on [also: you did not have to fight] horses or [other] mounts.

رَكُوب rakūb [quasi-pass. part. used collectively] riding animals (36:72) وَذَلَّلْنَٰهَا لَهُمْ فَمِنْهَا رَكُوبُهُمْ وَمِنْهَا يَأْكُلُونَ and We made them [the animals] manageable for them [humankind], so that from them they might [obtain] riding mounts, and from them they might obtain food.

entries.definition:
r–k–d to abate, to stagnate, to become still, to become sluggish,
stagnation, stillness. Of this root, رَوَاكِد rawākid occurs once in the Qur’an.

رَوَاكِد rawākid [pl. of fem. act. part. رَاكِدَة rāḳidatun] still, stagnant, motionless (42:33) إِن يَشَأْ يُسْكِنِ الرِّيحَ فَيَظْلَلْنَ رَوَاكِدَ عَلَى ظَهْرِهِ if He wills, He stills the wind, so that they [the ships] would lie motionless on its [the sea’s] back.
entries.definition_english:
r–k–d to abate, to stagnate, to become still, to become sluggish,
stagnation, stillness. Of this root, رَوَاكِد rawākid occurs once in the Qur’an.

رَوَاكِد rawākid [pl. of fem. act. part. رَاكِدَة rāḳidatun] still, stagnant, motionless (42:33) إِن يَشَأْ يُسْكِنِ الرِّيحَ فَيَظْلَلْنَ رَوَاكِدَ عَلَى ظَهْرِهِ if He wills, He stills the wind, so that they [the ships] would lie motionless on its [the sea’s] back.
